title: magma diver torment
wikitext:
[]: magma diver torment             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Oct 05, 2005 10:37 am this is the one and only thing in evangelion which just eludes me 100%. why the hell does Asuka not feel any pain when her eva's leg gets ripped off in magma diver? according to every other scenario in evangelion; what happens to the eva happens to the pilot, even if it's psychological.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:10 am Well, she did seem pretty pissed when it happened, and she was in full battle rush... They probably just fudged it because it wasn't convenient to the way they wanted the story to go. EDIT: Btw, THAT'S the only thing that eludes you? Christ, I wish I was you... :)

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:20 am Whose to say she didn't? Remember as well that the leg was cauterised upon being severed. Also Nerv have the ability to reduce synchronisation and thus ease the pilot pain somewhat.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Oct 05, 2005 11:36 am Plot hole?

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Wed Oct 05, 2005 4:38 pm I would say that the synch ratio was probably cut pretty low for the Magma Diver attempt, as Asuka couldn't really fight in that armor anyway. She'd only need synch for the arms, it's not like she's going to run anywhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Wed Oct 05, 2005 8:16 pm hax omg Haha, sorry, I couldn't resist. I support the beforementioned post that her sync ratio was simply cut down. Her eva was under serious pressure from the lava, along with the added discomfort of being submerged in molten lava. If her sync ratio wasn't cut down, she would have been in more pain/discomfort than she already was experiencing in the eva.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu Oct 06, 2005 10:53 am thanks for that, all makes sense now. EDIT: Btw, THAT'S the only thing that eludes you? Christ, I wish I was you...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Oct 06, 2005 5:52 pm This topic needs more looking into however. In paticular, this incident should be compared and contrasted with the other episodes of Eva-Pilot pain in the other episodes. Is there a general theory framework for the level of "synch" between pilot and Eva? Is there a suitable thread we can raise from the dead?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Oct 06, 2005 10:00 pm MAybe the eva pain thing wasn't important to the main story of the EP and Anno skiped over it for this one?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Fri Oct 07, 2005 10:49 am @ ObsessiveMathsFreak: Well, I haven't done this sort of thing before, but let's give it a go, eh? this is all off of the top of my head so it might not be too good, I might give it a bit more thought in the future: in episode #19 Shinji is throttled by unit-03. facing death by strangulation, fuyutski yells out something along the lines of "cut down the sync ratio to 65%" (here I must emphasise 'along the lines of') from what we can gather, this small change in sync would save Shinji from death even though he was being strongly strangled or at least enabling him to fight back without it doing Shinji more harm than good. In comparison to magma diver, this would show that Asuka would be perfectly safe from harm if the sync ratio was less than 50% , which wouldnâ€™t pose too much of a hindrance seen as she is just dangling and moving her arms. Evidence to show that asukaâ€™s sync ratio was below maximum is that her eva was much slower to react than normal, even taking into account the fact that sheâ€™s in a volcano. That canâ€™t be bad for a quick stab at the subject, surely?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 07, 2005 2:04 pm Yes you're probably right. Nerv's ability to control the level of synchronisation most likely has something to do with Asuka not passing out from pain or losing synchronisation as Rei did in episode #18. The big question here is why wasn't Rei's synchronisation lowered before Unit-00 arm was dismembered? Perhaps this is a delicate process that takes some seconds to complete. The synch perhaps cannot be let down too quickly with either perhaps damaging the pilot or perhaps simply causing the complete loss of the synchronisation? In episode #10, perhaps Asuka's synchronisation ratio was already reduced to help with the pain from the extreme heat and pressure, or perhaps was simply reduced enough that the pain did not cause the loss of synch or some other side effect.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Sat Oct 08, 2005 5:29 am i think that Rei's sync wasn't cut because of how shocked all of the people on the bridge were; even gendo stands up and shouts "NO". so they probably just didn't have time to take in the fact that they could stop her from nuking herself. as you said, Asuka's sync would have been cut down prior to her being submerged into the volcano so there were no problems with the time it would take.

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sat Oct 08, 2005 6:49 am It's gotta be the Synch-ratio reducement!! And yeah, maybe the reason for Rei's case is like what ice reaper said, It's pretty shocking for me too!!

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Sat Oct 08, 2005 1:07 pm There couldn't have been much time beween when Rei comes out and when Rei hits Zeruel with the N2 bomb. Zeruel does not seem to have very good reflexes and also seems to like to tank attacks a lot, as shown by how he withstands a long assault from Asuka before finally getting the toilet paper arms out and cashing her, and also by how he seems to have trouble mounting a counterattack against Shinji after his initial revenge shot. But still, if it took half a minute for Rei to get there, you'd expect Zeruel to blast her first. Edit: In addition, chances are that if they had tried to cut her synch ratio, it would have been cut around the time that Rei reached Zeruel. Cutting your Eva pilot's synch ratio just as she makes contact with the enemy is not a smart move.
